findings.location: conductor/workflow.md ~579 + build-ledger.md ~74 + workflow.md ~317 + Phase 5 blocked_stories ~2238
findings.description: story-terminal outcome enum lacks 'failed' value. SEAM-DISAGREE GUARD emits outcome:'failed' at ~579, but build-ledger.md enum is (merged|blocked|quarantined|contract-integrity-stop|stranded) — 'failed' absent. Rehydration blocked-routing set at ~317 ({blocked, quarantined, stranded, contract-integrity-stop}) excludes 'failed', so on resume a failed story is neither merged nor blocked. Phase 5 blocked_stories union also excludes it. Interrupt between the failed row and step 2.2's later blocked row makes the story invisible at end-gate.
findings.evidence: workflow.md ~579: { event: "story-terminal", story_slug: S.slug, outcome: "failed", reason: "seam-disagree: ..." }. build-ledger.md ~74: outcome (merged|blocked|quarantined|contract-integrity-stop|stranded). workflow.md ~317: If R.outcome in {"blocked", "quarantined", "stranded", "contract-integrity-stop"}: add R.story_slug to {{blocked}} — 'failed' absent. Phase 5 blocked_stories union (~2238) uses the same set.
findings.suggestion: Add 'failed' to the outcome enum in build-ledger.md ~74, to the rehydration routing set at ~317, and to the Phase 5 blocked_stories union. Alternative: have the seam-disagree guard emit outcome 'blocked' with a seam-disagree reason instead of 'failed'.

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md step 2.0 init block
findings.description: {{avfl_findings}} and {{e2e_findings}} are absent from the step 2.0 init block (13 accumulators initialized, including the new build_cross_artifact_notes) yet both are listed in the post-rehydration note as rehydrated accumulators. On a clean (non-resume) run, steps 3.3 and 4.2 append to never-initialized accumulators.
findings.evidence: Step 2.0 init block initializes 13 accumulators; {{avfl_findings}} and {{e2e_findings}} are not among them. Post-rehydration note lists both as rehydrated accumulators (rehydration routes at ~333-353 rebuild them from avfl-finding and e2e-stakes-escalation rows). Step 3.3 (~1779) and Phase 4 appends target these accumulators.
findings.suggestion: Add both to the step 2.0 init block: {{avfl_findings}} = [] and {{e2e_findings}} = [], each with a one-line comment: 'populated by step 3.3 / 4.2; rehydrated from ledger rows on resume'.

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md MAJOR-RESIDUAL guard (~2309-2311) + 5.RC.4 (~2531) vs e2e rehydration (~346-353)
findings.description: Incomplete e2e rehydration defeats the MAJOR-RESIDUAL guard on resume. The guard and the 5.RC.4 re-render read in-context {{avfl_findings}}/{{e2e_findings}}, but rehydration rebuilds {{e2e_findings}} ONLY from e2e-stakes-escalation rows ('the stakes portion'). Routine E2E residuals (triaged-out, severity major) are logged only as e2e-finding-auto-fixed rows — no severity, no finding_id, never rehydrated. On resume, a MAJOR triaged-out E2E residual escapes the guard INVARIANT ('No MAJOR-severity residual may leave this sprint without one'), violating NFR23 and the line-2200 completeness claim.
findings.evidence: Rehydration ~346-352: e2e-stakes-escalation route states it 'rehydrates the stakes portion of {{e2e_findings}}' — only event == 'e2e-stakes-escalation' rows are read; e2e-finding-auto-fixed rows are not rehydrated and carry no severity or finding_id. Guard source (c) (~2311) treats {{e2e_findings}} as complete. 5.RC.4 (~2531) re-renders from the same accumulators. Corroborated independently by STRUCT Adversary and COH Adversary.
findings.suggestion: Either (a) source the guard and 5.RC.4 residual checks from the ledger, adding severity + finding_id to the e2e-finding-auto-fixed row schema so routine residuals are durable and filterable, or (b) rehydrate the full {{e2e_findings}} (stakes + routine) by also reading e2e-finding-auto-fixed rows once enriched.

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md MAJOR-RESIDUAL guard source (b) (~2310-2326) vs stage3-finding-blocked row (~1128/1775)
findings.description: Guard source (b) filters on F.severity in {critical,major} and F.disposition NOT in {fixed,dismissed}, and passes F.summary to triage — but the stage3-finding-blocked row it reads carries only {story_slug, event, finding_id, finding_summary, attempts}: no severity field, no disposition field, and the summary field is named finding_summary. The severity filter cannot run, so a MAJOR blocked finding escapes the guard (partially mitigated by the block-time stub).
findings.evidence: Guard source (b) ~2310-2326 references F.severity, F.disposition, F.summary. stage3-finding-blocked writer (~1128) and schema confirm the row payload is {story_slug, event, finding_id, finding_summary, attempts} — none of the three referenced fields exist under those names.
findings.suggestion: Source guard (b) from finding-disposition ledger rows with disposition == 'blocked' (those rows now carry severity + summary), consistent with the ledger-first Phase 5 sourcing policy.

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md AVFL Phase 3 step 3.3 Group-A stage3-escalation append (~1779-1781) vs build-ledger.md ~927
findings.description: The AVFL Phase 3 (step 3.3) Group-A stage3-escalation ledger append omits evidence and suggested_fix. build-ledger.md ~927 claims 'Both append sites (2.S3 and AVFL Phase 3) produce this unified rich field set — Phase 5 Source 1 reads all fields unconditionally', but only the 2.S3 site (~1017) carries both fields. Downstream effect: Source-1-first dedup can keep the thin record over a richer Source-2 record, and end-gate decision cards lose evidence/suggested_fix for Group-A escalations. The Source 2 backstop via dedup softens but does not eliminate the impact.
findings.evidence: 2.S3 append site (~1017) includes evidence + suggested_fix. AVFL Phase 3 append (~1779-1781) omits both; the adjacent {{end_gate_escalations}} append at ~1775 has the L.evidence / L.suggestion bindings available. build-ledger.md ~927 unified-rich-field-set claim is therefore false for the Phase 3 site. Found independently by ACC Enumerator, COH Adversary, DOM Adversary, and DOM Enumerator.
findings.suggestion: Add evidence: L.evidence and suggested_fix: L.suggestion to the step 3.3 append (same bindings as the {{end_gate_escalations}} append at ~1775). Optionally also specify richer-record-wins dedup at the Phase 5 {{stakes_findings}} bind as a defensive measure.

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md Phase 5 Source 1 (~2201-2204) vs stage3-escalation producers (~1017, ~1779)
findings.description: Phase 5 Source 1 reads raw stage3-escalation ledger rows and asserts each entry carries 'summary' — but the rows carry 'finding_summary' (both producer sites and the schema agree). The renderer contract requires summary. This fires on the NORMAL path, not just resume. The rehydration route remaps correctly (summary: R.finding_summary) but Source 1 does not.
findings.evidence: Source 1 ~2204: 'Each entry carries: finding_id, stakes_class, timing_tier:"end-gate-expanded", summary, evidence, suggested_fix, story_slug.' Producer sites ~1017 and ~1779 write finding_summary; build-ledger.md schema confirms. Rehydration ~336 remaps summary: R.finding_summary; Source 1 has no remap. Found independently by ACC Adversary and DOM Enumerator.
findings.suggestion: Add a remap on the Source 1 read: summary: R.finding_summary (smaller change than renaming the row field across all producers and consumers).

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md step 2.S3 CASE fixed clean path (~1702-1703)
findings.description: On the CASE fixed clean path, the ledger append reads I.severity but I is never bound on that path. The accompanying note falsely claims 'the same lookup used above to validate stakes_class' — that guard reads F.stakes_class and binds nothing. All sibling cases (dismissed, triaged-out, escalated, blocked) have an explicit lookup binding I.
findings.evidence: ~1702-1703: append references I.severity with no preceding binding of I in the fixed-clean branch; the cited 'lookup above' validates F.stakes_class without binding I. Sibling branches each contain an explicit 'Look up the inbound finding I' step.
findings.suggestion: Add an explicit 'Look up the inbound finding I for F.finding_id in {{stage2_findings}} to recover severity' before the append, and correct the note to describe the actual binding.

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md Source 2/3 prohibitions (~2210/2214) vs guard (~2307-2312) + 5.RC.4 (~2529-2533) + rehydration rationale (~339)
findings.description: Two authoritative-store narratives coexist. Source 2 and Source 3 each state 'do NOT read from {{avfl_findings}}/{{e2e_findings}} in-context — the durable ledger rows are the authoritative source', while the MAJOR-RESIDUAL guard and 5.RC.4 deliberately read those same in-context accumulators (the rehydration note at ~339 exists precisely 'so the guard and 5.RC.4 can read non-empty accumulators'). 5.RC.4 additionally claims its accumulator-based re-assembly uses 'the same logic as the initial report-build action', which is ledger-based — the two cannot both be the same logic. The narrative contradiction is the root that makes CONS3-003's rehydration gap dangerous: a reader following the ledger-first rule would be safe; a reader following the accumulator path inherits the incomplete rehydration.
findings.evidence: Source 2 ~2210: 'do NOT read from {{avfl_findings}} in-context accumulator — it is a volatile cache'. Source 3 ~2214: same for {{e2e_findings}}. Rehydration ~339-341: 'This rehydrates {{avfl_findings}} so the MAJOR-RESIDUAL GOVERNANCE GUARD (Phase 5 approve) and the 5.RC.4 re-render can read non-empty accumulators on resume'. 5.RC.4 ~2529-2531: 'Re-assemble {{stakes_findings}} from {{end_gate_escalations}}, {{avfl_findings}}, and {{e2e_findings}}' under 'same logic as the initial report-build action'. Found independently by ACC Adversary and COH Adversary.
findings.suggestion: Pick one store. Preferred: make the guard and 5.RC.4 ledger-sourced (truly the same logic as the initial report build), retiring the accumulator reads. Alternative: state the accumulators-are-rehydrated-equivalent-view rule once, with explicit reconciliation notes at both consumers (~2210/2214 and the guard/5.RC.4).

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md step 3.D (~1878-1997) vs rehydration avfl-finding route (~333-344)
findings.description: Crash mid-3.D produces duplicate {{avfl_findings}} entries on resume. Rehydration loads prior 3.D avfl-finding rows into {{avfl_findings}}; 3.D then re-runs (it has no phase checkpoint of its own) and re-appends the same entries in-context. {{ledger_seen_events}} covers only finding-disposition and stage3-escalation rows, so it does not gate avfl-finding appends. The MAJOR-RESIDUAL guard reads {{avfl_findings}}, so duplicate decision stubs are possible.
findings.evidence: Rehydration ~333-344 appends every avfl-finding row to {{avfl_findings}}. 3.D (~1878-1997) appends in-context with no already-present check. ~355: {{ledger_seen_events}} is built from rows but its duplicate-prevention usage covers finding-disposition + stage3-escalation events only.
findings.suggestion: Before the 3.D in-context append, skip entries whose finding_id is already present in {{avfl_findings}} (rehydrated); or extend {{ledger_seen_events}} to cover avfl-finding rows and gate both the ledger and in-context writes.

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md rehydration stage3-mid-flight-escalation clause (~287-292) vs writer (~1076)
findings.description: Rehydration reads R.resolution and R.stakes_class for stage3-mid-flight-escalation rows, but the writer emits only {story_slug, event, disposition, timing_tier, finding_count} (schema in build-ledger.md ~928 confirms). Rehydrated entries carry undefined resolution/stakes_class.
findings.evidence: Rehydration clause ~287-292 references R.resolution and R.stakes_class. Writer ~1076 and build-ledger.md ~928 schema show neither field in the row payload.
findings.suggestion: Split the rehydration for the two event variants: read resolution only for mid-flight-escalation rows that carry it; omit or null resolution/stakes_class for the stage3 variant.

findings.location: build-ledger.md [WIRED] note (~937) vs conductor/workflow.md step 2.2 (~1263-1268)
findings.description: The [WIRED] note falsely claims 'step 2.2 also appends each entry to the build ledger as a stage3-escalation row' — step 2.2 accumulation makes no ledger append; the actual writes are at 2.S3 (~1017) and 3.3 (~1779). An executing LLM following the note may double-append.
findings.evidence: build-ledger.md ~937 [WIRED] note names step 2.2 as an append site. workflow.md ~1263-1268 (step 2.2) only accumulates in-context; no ledger write present. Actual write sites verified at ~1017 and ~1779.
findings.suggestion: Correct the note to name the two actual write sites (2.S3 and AVFL Phase 3 step 3.3).

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md AVFL Phase 3 avfl-finding write loop (~1760-1762) vs Group-A re-tag (~1782-1784)
findings.description: avfl-finding ledger rows are written BEFORE the Group-A re-tag flips disposition residual→escalated, so durable rows keep stale disposition 'residual' for Group-A findings. Source 2's filter accepts both dispositions so there is no runtime impact, but the durable record is wrong and the rationale note at ~1782-1784 is now misleading. Recalibrated from low to minor: a durable ledger row carrying a stale disposition is a data-integrity defect in the authoritative store, even if current consumers tolerate it.
findings.evidence: Write loop at ~1760-1762 precedes the re-tag at ~1782-1784. Source 2 (~2206-2208) filters disposition in {'residual','escalated'}, masking the staleness at runtime.
findings.suggestion: Move the write loop after the re-tag, or write Group-A rows with disposition 'escalated' directly; update the rationale note accordingly.

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md stage-2 REVIEWER B blocks (dedicated-run ~686-695, covered-by-composition ~720s)
findings.description: Circular canonicity: each REVIEWER B block designates the OTHER as 'the single canonical statement'. The dedicated-run block says the covered-by-composition block is canonical; the covered-by-composition block says the dedicated-run block is canonical. Behavioral content is identical, so this is doc-integrity only.
findings.evidence: Dedicated-run block: 'the covered-by-composition block is the single canonical statement'. Covered-by-composition block: 'that block [dedicated-run] is the single canonical REVIEWER B specification.'
findings.suggestion: Pick one canonical home (dedicated-run) and collapse the other block to a one-line deferral pointing at it.

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md SEAM-DISAGREE reason string (~580)
findings.description: The reason string asserts 'producer delivered an already-committed change' definitively; tip-advance could theoretically have other causes (rebase, external mutation). Recalibrated from major (COH Enumerator) to minor: the string is diagnostic narrative in a ledger row, not control flow — the guard triggers on tip-advance regardless, and the worktree-clean bool is preserved. Impact is limited to a potentially misleading debug message.
findings.evidence: workflow.md ~580: reason: 'seam-disagree: consumer expects uncommitted working-tree changes plus a file list; producer delivered an already-committed change — history tip advanced beyond launch base (worktree clean: <bool>)'. Surrounding text (~575-576) already concedes the guard fires on tip-advance regardless of cause.
findings.suggestion: Soften to 'likely indicates the dev agent committed changes' while keeping the worktree-clean bool.

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md standing rule KEY VOCABULARY vs CARVE-OUT (same block)
findings.description: The absolute 'any {{build_log}}' prohibition reads as contradicting the carve-out in the same block; a reader hits the prohibition before learning the exception exists.
findings.evidence: KEY VOCABULARY states an absolute prohibition; the CARVE-OUT later in the same block qualifies it without a forward reference from the prohibition sentence.
findings.suggestion: Add a forward reference in the prohibition sentence: '(see CARVE-OUT below ...)'.

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md step 2.0 post-rehydration summary note
findings.description: The post-rehydration summary note omits {{ledger_seen_events}} from its accumulator inventory, although it IS built during rehydration ('Track seen events', ~355-356).
findings.evidence: workflow.md ~355-356: 'Track seen events: build a set of (story_slug, event, finding_id) tuples from all rows. Bind {{ledger_seen_events}} = this set'. The summary note's inventory does not list it.
findings.suggestion: Add {{ledger_seen_events}} to the post-rehydration inventory list.

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md step 2.S3 escalated-case parenthetical note
findings.description: The escalated-case stage3-escalation parenthetical note lists finding_id/stakes_class/evidence/suggested_fix but omits finding_summary, which the append also carries.
findings.evidence: Note enumerates four fields; the append at the 2.S3 site (~1017) also writes finding_summary per the build-ledger schema.
findings.suggestion: Add finding_summary to the note's field list.

findings.location: build-ledger.md coverage-discharge-consumer-complete (Coverage Events table + Conductor-Level Events table)
findings.description: The event appears in BOTH the Coverage Events table (story_slug required implied) and the Conductor-Level Events table (story_slug may be null) — contradictory placement for the same event's schema.
findings.evidence: build-ledger.md lists coverage-discharge-consumer-complete in two tables with differing story_slug nullability implications.
findings.suggestion: Add a cross-note in the Coverage Events table row pointing at the Conductor-Level entry and stating the null-slug condition.

findings.location: build-ledger.md stage3-escalation event categorization
findings.description: stage3-escalation is categorized as a Per-Story event, but the AVFL Phase 3 site may write sentinel story_slug 'sprint-integration', which is not a story.
findings.evidence: build-ledger.md taxonomy places stage3-escalation under Per-Story events; the Phase 3 producer can emit story_slug 'sprint-integration'.
findings.suggestion: Add a note on the stage3-escalation row documenting the sentinel value.

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md step 2.S3 INVOCATION CONTEXT
findings.description: 'Stage-2 callers' is imprecise now that stage-2 is split into two dispatch paths (dedicated-run and covered-by-composition).
findings.evidence: INVOCATION CONTEXT says 'Stage-2 callers'; stage-2 now dispatches REVIEWER A on the dedicated-run path and REVIEWER B on both paths.
findings.suggestion: Reword: 'Stage-2 reviewers (REVIEWER A on the dedicated-run path, REVIEWER B on both paths) ...'.

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md step 2.1.2a
findings.description: Letter-suffix naming (2.1.2a) is an outlier among N.N.N siblings. Cosmetic.
findings.evidence: Sibling steps use pure numeric N.N.N numbering; 2.1.2a is the only letter-suffixed step in the run.
findings.suggestion: Renumber to a pure numeric step or document the suffix convention.

findings.location: conductor/workflow.md step 2.C Path B deferral note
findings.description: Deferral note field runs 3 sentences where peers use one clause. Verbose outlier.
findings.evidence: Path B note is three sentences; sibling deferral notes are single clauses.
findings.suggestion: Trim to one clause matching peer format.

findings.location: dev/workflow.md DEV COMMIT AUTHORITY
findings.description: 'unstaged/staged-but-not-committed' is an ambiguous register for the allowed working-tree states.
findings.evidence: dev/workflow.md DEV COMMIT AUTHORITY uses the compound 'unstaged/staged-but-not-committed' phrasing.
findings.suggestion: Reword: 'changes may be unstaged or staged, but must not be committed'.

findings.location: sprint specs (planning artifact) — spec supersession tags
findings.description: Supersession tags placed inline in table cells rather than using the blockquote affordance. Cosmetic; planning artifact only.
findings.evidence: Supersession tags appear inline in table cells, diverging from the blockquote convention used elsewhere.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on the blockquote affordance for supersession notes, or leave as-is (planning artifact).
